first_paragraph: ***Love, Life and Laughter*** is a 1923 British [silent film](/wiki/Silent_film "Silent film"), written and directed by [George Pearson](/wiki/George_Pearson_(filmmaker) "George Pearson (filmmaker)"). For many years the film was thought lost, and was listed as one of the [British Film Institute](/wiki/British_Film_Institute "British Film Institute")'s "[75 Most Wanted](/wiki/BFI_75_Most_Wanted "BFI 75 Most Wanted")" lost films.[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2) On 2 April 2014, Dutch film institute [Eye](/wiki/EYE_Film_Institute_Netherlands "EYE Film Institute Netherlands") announced it had discovered a copy.[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

1923 film

| Love, Life and Laughter | |
| --- | --- |
| Directed by | [George Pearson](/wiki/George_Pearson_(filmmaker) "George Pearson (filmmaker)") |
| Written by | George Pearson |
| Starring | [Betty Balfour](/wiki/Betty_Balfour "Betty Balfour") |
| Cinematography | [Percy Strong](/wiki/Percy_Strong "Percy Strong") |
| Release date | * June 1923 (1923-06) |
| Running time | 90 minutes |
| Country | United Kingdom |
| Language | Silent |

***Love, Life and Laughter*** is a 1923 British [silent film](/wiki/Silent_film "Silent film"), written and directed by [George Pearson](/wiki/George_Pearson_(filmmaker) "George Pearson (filmmaker)"). For many years the film was thought lost, and was listed as one of the [British Film Institute](/wiki/British_Film_Institute "British Film Institute")'s "[75 Most Wanted](/wiki/BFI_75_Most_Wanted "BFI 75 Most Wanted")" lost films.[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2) On 2 April 2014, Dutch film institute [Eye](/wiki/EYE_Film_Institute_Netherlands "EYE Film Institute Netherlands") announced it had discovered a copy.[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## Plot

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Love,_Life_and_Laughter_(1923_film)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Plot")]

An impoverished author and a cabaret girl each have their dream of success, but are happy to wake to each other and reality.
